items.q: What does insulin do to blood glucose?
items.correct: It lowers blood glucose by causing cells (especially liver and muscle) to take it up and store it as glycogen.
items.misconception: Insulin raises blood glucose to give the body energy.
items.random_wrong: Insulin destroys glucose in the bloodstream so it cannot be used.
items.why: Students associate sugar with energy and 'doing something with sugar' with raising energy, missing that insulin removes glucose from blood.

items.q: What is the role of the guide RNA in CRISPR-Cas9?
items.correct: It directs Cas9 to the specific DNA sequence to cut by base-pairing with the target.
items.misconception: Guide RNA is the new sequence that gets inserted into the genome.
items.random_wrong: Guide RNA is the enzyme that cuts the DNA.
items.why: Students confuse the guide RNA (which targets) with Cas9 (which cuts) and with any donor DNA (which is inserted) — three distinct components.

items.q: What is the role of DNA ligase when inserting a gene into a plasmid?
items.correct: It forms phosphodiester bonds that join the gene to the cut plasmid, sealing the recombinant DNA.
items.misconception: DNA ligase cuts the plasmid open so the gene can be inserted.
items.random_wrong: DNA ligase reads the gene and translates it into protein.
items.why: Students confuse the cutting enzymes (restriction endonucleases) with the joining enzyme (ligase); both are needed but do opposite jobs.

items.q: Which enzyme breaks protein into amino acids?
items.why: It's Protease. “Amylase” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: Which test turns blue-black if starch is present?
items.why: Correct answer: Iodine. “Benedict's” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: Bile is released into the small intestine. The stomach contents arrive at about pH 2. After bile, intestinal pH is around what value?
items.why: The real figure is 8. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
